title: Darshan Hiranandani : How to Trigger Automation 1.5 Hours Before a Date Column Time?

posts.author: darshanhira24 (Darshan Hiranandani)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

I’m Darshan Hiranandani looking for a way to trigger an automation based on a time relative to a date column. Specifically, I want to trigger an automation 1.5 hours before the time in a particular date column.

The date-based automation triggers I’ve seen so far seem to only allow you to select a fixed time for every occurrence of the automation, but my use case requires more flexibility. I need the automation to trigger dynamically based on the value in the date column.

It seems like this might not be possible, but I’m hoping someone here has a workaround or knows a better approach. Has anyone tried something like this or found a solution?

Thanks in advance for any suggestions!

Regards
Darshan Hiranandani

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @darshanhira24,
Hey! While this isn’t a native feature in monday yet, you can use the Advanced Time Triggers app to set up actions based on dates and times saved in a date column.

image1035×407 66.3 KB

The triggers are available in the automation builder, allowing you to create custom automations to trigger any action you need…

For more information you can ready this community topic its work around the same topic;

Delays with automations Platform discussions
Hello. I’m experiencing significant delays when running automations. For example, I have an automation "When Status changes set Another Status to some value’. And when I change the Status column, it takes 5-10+ seconds for this automation to run and to set the Another Status value. Sometimes, the Another Status value never changes until I manually refresh the browser tab. Same goes for other change-triggered automations (moving to the group, setting dates, etc). Is that a general/known proble…

Zaki
Business Workflow Consultant
Ready Logic
